notes_md:
**nhau** means *each other* — it shows a mutual action between two or more people.

It usually comes **after a verb** to show that both sides do the action to the other. You need at least two people in the context; you can’t use it for just one person.  


It’s only used for actions between people (not objects). A close English idea is “each other” or sometimes “one another.”
sentenceBreakdowns: Anh (you) + và (and) + em (I) + gặp (meet) + nhau (each other)
Chị (she) + và (and) + em (I) + giúp (help) + nhau (each other)
Anh (he) + và (and) + chị (she) + yêu (love) + nhau (each other)
exampleSentencesTranslated: You and I meet each other
She and I help each other
He and she love each other

example.en: We go for a walk together.
example.es: Vamos a dar un paseo juntos.
example.vi: Chúng ta cùng nhau đi dạo.

notes:
nhau means each other — it shows a mutual action between two or more people.

It usually comes after a verb to show that both sides do the action to the other. You need at least two people in the context; you can’t use it for just one person.  

It’s only used for actions between people (not objects). A close English idea is “each other” or sometimes “one another.”
